title: Bridge Essay: Learning to Be Conscious.
concept: 4E cognition — embodied, enactive, extended, embedded mind.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, enactive, extended, embedded mind.

**Learning to Be Conscious.** - Trends Cogn Sci (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2019.11.011. PMID: 31892458. Consciousness remains a formidable challenge. Different theories of consciousness have proposed vastly different mechanisms to account for phenomenal experience. Here, appealing to aspects of global workspace theory, higher-order theories, social theories, and predictive processing, we introduce a novel framework: the self-organizing metarerpresentational account (SOMA), in which consciousness is viewed as something that the brain learns to do.
